channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: The discourse on cat’s tastes
item.description:
Source: here.

As I was in the embrace of the yakShini she asked what is so funny about the cat’s taste. I said: Most mammals sense 5 tastes with 7-transmembrane receptors with extracellular PBP domains. This kind of receptor seems to be an ancient acquisition of the animal lineage from bacteria. Thus our sense of taste seems to have emerged directly from a bacterial sense of taste directly fed into our neurons.+++(5)+++ The cats interestingly have lost their sweet receptor and cannot sense the sweet taste.+++(5)+++ The emergence of hyper-carnivory in the cats appears to have made their need to appreciate sweet superflous for all that mattered was taste for raw meat, and not sweet. Thus, the cats lost their ability to sense sweet.

item.title: Japan lion
item.description:
Source: TW

Did Japan have tigers or lions?

Fossils of a big cat had been found in Japan, and people assumed they were the now extinct Japanese tiger. However, Chinese workers recently sequenced the mitogenomes and partial proteomic fragments of 5 Japanese “tigers,” and it turned out they were lions, not tigers.

The aDNA investigations and other investigations reveal that there were 5 types of lions: 1. The basal-most maned Panthera leo; 2. The American lion Panthera atrox; 3. The European lion, Panthera fossilis; 4. Type-I cave lion, Panthera spelaea-1; 5. Type-II cave lion, Panthera spelaea-2. The cave lions lacked manes.
